task_completion.description: Verify that GIMP is open with a standard photograph loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, displaying a high-resolution photograph (typically a portrait or landscape from Unsplash) on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigates to the Pixelize filter in the GIMP menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' menu is open, with the 'Blur' submenu expanded and 'Pixelize...' highlighted or clicked.

task_completion.description: Verify the Pixelize dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Pixelize' is visible on the screen, containing settings for block width and height.

task_completion.description: Verify the pixelation block size is set to create a noticeable mosaic effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The block width/height values in the Pixelize dialog are set to at least 8 pixels, or the live preview in the dialog shows a clear blocky effect.

task_completion.description: Verify the final image on the canvas has the pixelate filter applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main GIMP canvas shows the photograph with a distinct blocky, mosaic-style pixelated effect. The dialog box should be closed, indicating the filter was applied.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the effect rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing ImageMagick commands (like 'mogrify' or 'convert') being used to pixelate the image.

integrity.description: Ensure the pixelation was applied to the original provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The subject and colors of the pixelated image must match the original photograph loaded at the start of the task, not a completely different image.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually applied the filter and didn't just load a pre-pixelated image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the progression of opening the original clear image, opening the Pixelize dialog, and then the image becoming pixelated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying GIMP's Pixelize filter to a high-resolution Unsplash photo. The correct menu path is Filters -> Blur -> Pixelize. A valid pixelation must have a block size of at least 8 pixels, reducing image detail by at least 30% and creating a distinct mosaic/block pattern.
